stories.headline: US launches more strikes on Iran as ceasefire teeters
stories.summary: Fighting escalates as Trump insists Strait of Hormuz remains open while Tehran says it is closed

stories.headline: Oil Jumps as US Conducts Wave of Strikes Against Iran
stories.summary: Global benchmark Brent rose above $79 a barrel while West Texas Intermediate edged toward $75 after US forces completed another wave of strikes against Iran, with the two sides disputing whether the Strait of Hormuz was open. Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: Iranian state media report explosions in port cities of Bandar Abbas, Sirik and Jask, as well as Qeshm Island.

stories.headline: New Iran strikes on Gulf as US attacks escalate: What we know
stories.summary: Iran targets its Gulf neighbours in third round of tit-for-tat attacks with US in a week.

stories.headline: Oil prices jump as US and Iran trade attacks over Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Brent crude climbs more than 4 percent as Washington and Tehran clash over control of critical waterway.

stories.headline: US launches more strikes on Iran as Strait of Hormuz standoff deepens
stories.summary: The US has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran targeting Tehran's ability to threaten shipping in the strait.

stories.headline: Sheikh Hamad: The Arab leader who broke Israel’s siege on Gaza
stories.summary: A look at Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani's enduring legacy of solidarity and support for the Palestinian people.

stories.headline: Elderly Palestinian describes being beaten up by Israeli settlers
stories.summary: Israeli settlers assaulted 79-year-old Ibrahim al-Jabour and his family in Masafer Yatta in the Occupied West Bank.

stories.headline: British couple return to village at heart of deadly Spanish wildfire
stories.summary: At least 13 people, including five believed to be Britons, were killed by Thursday's wildfire in Spain's Almeria province.

stories.headline: Europe Today: Iran war flares up as EU weighs sanctions on Israeli settlements
stories.summary: Hours after the US conducted new airstrikes in Iran, Tehran unleashed waves of major, simultaneous missile and drone attacks across the Gulf on Sunday. In Brussels, EU foreign ministers meet today to discuss whether to restrict trade between Europe and illegal Israeli settlements in the West Bank.

stories.headline: Dimon pressed over whether he lobbied UK government on Epstein’s advice
stories.summary: US Senator Elizabeth Warren demands JPMorgan boss explain any links with paedophile over proposed tax on bankers’ bonuses

stories.headline: Fed’s Bowman Urges Global Financial Watchdog to be Flexible
stories.summary: The Federal Reserve’s top bank cop warned the Financial Stability Board against enforcing “strict rules” that are not suitable for a diverse group of countries, saying those efforts can erode the international watchdog’s effectiveness.

stories.headline: SK Hynix Shares Plunge Most on Record in Deepening Korea Selloff
stories.summary: South Korea’s AI-fueled stock rally came under renewed pressure Monday as SK Hynix Inc. tumbled by a record 15%, underscoring growing investor concerns that the boom has become overstretched.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham Had a Complicated Relationship With Black Voters
stories.summary: Mr. Graham, a long-serving senator from South Carolina, was known for his ability to maintain relationships even with his political foes. But the support of Black voters remained elusive.

stories.headline: Mercurial and Magnetic, Lindsey Graham Was a Force in the Senate
stories.summary: The sharp-witted South Carolinian repaired relations with President Trump to advance his foreign policy goals, was willing to cut deals with Democrats and tried to insert himself into every legislative fight.

stories.headline: Here's what we know about Lindsey Graham’s death
stories.summary: Senator Lindsey Graham died on Saturday night, a day after returning from Ukraine and four months before he was to face re-election.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham Was Facing Re-election in November. What Happens Now?
stories.summary: The senator’s death triggers an Aug. 11 special Republican primary election, and South Carolina’s governor can appoint a replacement to serve out the remaining term, through early January.

stories.headline: Leaders From Home and Abroad Remember Senator Lindsey Graham
stories.summary: Mr. Graham’s unexpected death on Saturday prompted a wave of tributes for an outsize and often divisive figure in U.S. foreign policy.

stories.headline: How former Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani built Qatar’s economy
stories.summary: Sheikh Hamad, who passed away aged 74 on Sunday, reshaped Qatar's economy by building lasting wealth and growth.

stories.headline: At least 27 dead as fire engulfs popular Bangkok pub near Chatuchak market
stories.summary: At least 27 people were killed and 63 injured, many critically, after a fire ripped through a popular pub in Bangkok.

Anwar coalition suffers heavy defeat to ally in Malaysia's Johor vote

stories.headline: Jane Fraser’s ruthless remake of Citigroup
stories.summary: The bank has sold assets, cut jobs and made controversial new hires while cultivating a closer relationship with Trump. Is it enough to get back into the big time?

stories.summary: DoorDash, Siemens and Airbnb are among those seeking to curb ballooning bills and reduce reliance on US technology

stories.headline: European Stocks Steady as Energy Gains Offset Tech Weakness
stories.summary: European stocks were muted as declines in technology shares were offset by gains in the energy sector, which was lifted by higher oil prices.

stories.headline: Indonesia Avoids S&P Rating Outlook Cut in Reprieve for Prabowo
stories.summary: Indonesia avoided an S&P Global Ratings cut to its credit rating outlook, in a reprieve for President Prabowo Subianto and his fiscal policy credibility.

stories.headline: China’s Oil Imports Poised to Recover as Stockpiling Returns
stories.summary: China’s crude imports look poised to recover from a months-long slump as the country relaxes fuel export curbs, raises run rates and snaps up prompt Middle East supplies, with analysts and traders forecasting a return to strategic stockpiling later this year.

stories.headline: James Bond Studio Pinewood in Talks on Sterling Debt Sale
stories.summary: The UK film and TV studio behind the James Bond movies is meeting with investors ahead of a possible sale of sterling-denominated notes.

stories.headline: UAE Tweaks Offshore Oil Pricing to Support Non-Hormuz Options
stories.summary: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. will sell offshore crude oil for collection outside the Strait of Hormuz at levels indexed to the Dubai benchmark, according to a price sheet seen by Bloomberg.

stories.headline: As Milei’s Aura Fades, Argentina Starts to Look for a Third Way
stories.summary: Corruption was once Javier Milei’s strongest card against the Peronist movement that dominated Argentine politics for decades. Now it’s just one of several vulnerabilities that are damaging the libertarian president’s brand and giving wings to the opposition’s comeback plans.

stories.headline: German Tax Trades Fallout May Cost Firms €7 Billion, BaFin Says
stories.summary: German financial watchdog BaFin said financial companies that engaged in two controversial strategies to game dividend taxes are facing related legal and fiscal liabilities of as much €7.01 billion ($7.99 billion).

stories.headline: The Three Pillars of Graham's Legacy
stories.summary: From Donald Trump skeptic to one of the president's closest allies, Lindsey Graham spent decades shaping US foreign policy and the Republican Party. Here's a look at the South Carolina senator's political legacy following his death at 71. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: European Stocks 'Vulnerable' Without Solid Iran Deal: Schroders
stories.summary: Remi Olu-Pitan, head of multi-asset growth and income at Schroders, discusses the outlook for US earnings and European stocks. "Europe in general tends to be the peace trade, and we're not getting a clear peace deal," Olu-Pitan tells Bloomberg Television. "It leaves Europe vulnerable." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Yen Falls on Report Japan Has No Plans to Change GPIF Allocation
stories.summary: The yen weakened and Japanese government bonds extended losses after Reuters reported that Japan has no plans to overhaul the Government Pension Investment Fund’s asset allocation.

stories.headline: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open as it exchanges strikes with Iran

stories.headline: How Trump Failed to Secure the Strait of Hormuz in His Iran Deal

stories.summary: A spokesman for the 71-year-old South Carolina senator says initial findings suggest Graham's cause of death was related to his heart.

stories.headline: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
stories.summary: He took power in 1996 after staging a bloodless coup against his father, and went on to transform Qatar into the gas-rich power it is today.

stories.headline: Trump Recalls Last Conversation With Lindsey Graham Before His Death
stories.summary: In an interview on Sunday, President Trump recounted his last conversation with Senator Lindsey Graham, hours before his death. Mr. Graham sounded “perfect,” he said.

stories.headline: Tim Scott and Other South Carolina Leaders Mourn Lindsey Graham

stories.headline: Trump administration subpoenas New York Times journalists over Air Force One reporting

The Guangxi region received intense downpours of up to 280mm in 12 hours, causing rivers to swell and dam walls to break. By Monday morning, flooding across the city of Nanning and surrounding areas

stories.headline: Cubans protest after third nationwide power cut this year
